title: Compose recomposition model
description: Composables are functions of state that the runtime re-invokes when the state they read changes; UI is described, not mutated.
body: Use when reasoning about Compose updates. A composable describes UI for the current state; when an observable state it reads changes, the runtime recomposes (re-invokes) the affected composables and skips the rest. Recomposition can happen often, in any order, and be skipped, so composables must be side-effect-free and idempotent in their body. Do not perform side effects directly in composition or rely on a plain var. Actions: use State for data, side-effect APIs (LaunchedEffect) for effects, and keep parameters stable. Validation signals include updates from state and minimal recomposition. Risk boundary: side effects in the composable body run unpredictably on every recomposition, and non-State data never triggers an update — the observable-state model is mandatory for correctness.
